subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

key_points: Indian nationalism developed gradually in the late nineteenth century
The Indian National Congress became the main organization leading the freedom struggle
Moderates demanded reforms through petitions and constitutional methods
Extremist leaders demanded Swaraj and encouraged stronger political actions
The Swadeshi Movement started after the Partition of Bengal in 1905
Gandhi introduced nonviolent mass movements that involved large sections of society
examples: Moderate leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji used economic criticism to expose British rule
Extremist leaders like Bal Gangadhar Tilak encouraged mass protests
Swadeshi Movement encouraged use of Indian goods and boycott of British goods
The Non-Cooperation Movement asked people to boycott British institutions
The Civil Disobedience Movement involved breaking colonial laws peacefully
important_terms: Moderates – Early Congress leaders who used peaceful methods
Extremists – Leaders who demanded stronger political action and immediate Swaraj
Drain of Wealth – Theory explaining economic exploitation of India by Britain
Non-Cooperation – Refusal to cooperate with colonial government
Civil Disobedience – Peacefully breaking unjust laws
exam_tips: Understand causes and results of major movements
Remember important leaders and their contributions
Explain differences between Moderates and Extremists clearly
Use examples like Swadeshi Movement and Salt March when writing answers
Write short answers using clear historical terms
